classFeature.name: Mana Capacity
entries: Your body can store up to 10 + your Constitution modifier + your proficiency bonus mana.

Your maximum mana capacity is determined after you finish a long rest; temporary changes to your Constitution modifier, such as from your Mana Enhancement feature or other magical effects, do not alter your maximum mana until you complete your next long rest.

classFeature.name: Mana Crystal
entries: Your training centers around a specially prepared crystal focus that serves as both your spellcasting focus and a reservoir for your magical energy.

entries.name: Crystal Charges
entries: The Mana Crystal has a maximum number of charges equal to your current maximum mana capacity. When you finish a long rest, the crystal regains all expended charges.

entries.name: Crystal Properties
entries: The crystal's appearance reflects your philosophical balance, typically featuring patterns that represent harmony between mind and body. The crystal glows softly when containing charges, with intensity reflecting available charge capacity. If lost or destroyed, you can craft a replacement during a long rest using materials worth 50 gp.

classFeature.name: Spellcasting
entries: Your ability to absorb and manipulate magical energy allows you to cast spells.

entries.name: Spellcasting Ability
entries: Wisdom is your spellcasting ability for your thaumaphage spells. You use your Wisdom whenever a spell refers to your spellcasting ability.
{@b Spell save DC} = 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Wisdom modifier
{@b Spell attack bonus} = your proficiency bonus + your Wisdom modifier

entries.name: Learning Spells
entries: Your ability to cast spells beyond the {@spell Convert Essence|VeiledOmensThaumaphage} cantrip and the {@spell Ether Burn|VeiledOmensThaumaphage} spell depends on the Devouring Art you choose at 2nd level. Some arts grant you methods to learn and cast a variety of spells.

entries.name: Cantrips
entries: At 1st level, you know the {@spell Convert Essence|VeiledOmensThaumaphage} cantrip.

entries.name: Spells Known
entries: At 1st level, you know the {@spell Ether Burn|VeiledOmensThaumaphage} spell.

entries.name: Direct Casting
entries: You cast spells by expending mana directly. You do not use spell slots.
You can cast spells at higher levels by paying the mana cost of the higher level.

entries.name: Overexertion
entries: The Thaumaphage table shows your Mana Exertion Limit, the maximum amount of mana you can spend on a single spell without risk. If you cast a spell by expending an amount of mana that exceeds this limit, immediately after the spell is cast you must make a Constitution saving throw with a DC equal to 10 + the amount of mana spent over your limit. On a failed save, you gain one level of exhaustion.

classFeature.name: Siphon Magic
entries: Your study of thaumic absorption allows you to draw out and internalize the magical energy stored within charged objects.

As an action, you can siphon energy from a magic item you are holding or wearing that uses charges. When you do so, you expend a number of charges from the item and regain a corresponding amount of mana.

items: {@b Mana Crystal:} When siphoning from your own Mana Crystal, you regain 1 mana for each charge you expend from the crystal.
{@b Other Magic Items:} When siphoning from any other magic item, you regain 1 mana for every 2 charges you expend, rounded down.

entries: You can siphon a maximum number of charges at one time equal to your proficiency bonus. The mana you regain cannot exceed your maximum mana capacity.

classFeature.name: Devouring Arts
entries: At 2nd level, you choose an art that shapes how you process and utilize absorbed magical energy. These specialized disciplines dictate how you metabolize, transform, and master the potent magical energies you take in, shaping them to your will.
Your choice grants you features at 2nd level and again at 6th, 10th, and 14th levels.

entries.caption: Devouring Arts
rows: Absorbed mana becomes learned spell knowledge.
Absorbed magical energy becomes enhanced bodily capability.

classFeature.name: Consume Magic
entries: Starting at 3rd level, your mastery over absorption deepens, allowing you to intercept and devour hostile magical effects.

When you are targeted by a spell or other magical effect, you can use your reaction to attempt to negate and absorb it. The magical effect is nullified for you, and you regain mana equal to your proficiency bonus.
You can use this ability twice, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: At 4th level, you can increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1 each. You can't increase an ability score above 20.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: At 8th level, you can increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1 each. You can't increase an ability score above 20.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: At 12th level, you can increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1 each. You can't increase an ability score above 20.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: At 16th level, you can increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1 each. You can't increase an ability score above 20.

classFeature.name: Mana Shift
entries: Starting at 17th level, your attunement to magical energies allows you to briefly become intangible, letting hostile magic pass through you.

When you would be affected by an instantaneous magical effect, such as a {@spell fireball} spell, a {@spell hold person} spell, or a dragon's breath weapon, you can use your reaction and expend 5 mana to momentarily phase into an incorporeal state. If you do so, the triggering instantaneous magical effect fails to affect you.

classFeature.name: True Devourer
entries: At 20th level, you achieve perfect harmony with the flow of magic, able to draw upon its deepest currents and perceive its true nature. You gain the following benefits:

items: {@b Empowered Mana:} Your maximum mana capacity increases by an amount equal to your Wisdom modifier. Additionally, when you roll initiative and have no mana remaining, you regain mana equal to your Wisdom modifier, minimum 1.

{@b Thaumic Sight:} You can use an action to activate this sight for 1 minute. While active, you gain truesight out to a range of 60 feet. Additionally, you can see a faint aura around any visible creature or object in the area that bears magic, and you learn its school of magic, if any. You can discern the presence of magical traps or hidden magical effects within this range. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Mana Shaping
entries: Starting when you choose this art at 2nd level, when you are targeted by a spell, whether cast by a spellcaster or originating from a magic item, or an innate magical ability from a creature, you can use your reaction to attempt to understand and learn its magical signature. This attempt is made after the effects of the spell or ability have been resolved. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

To learn the spell or innate magical ability, you must make a Wisdom (Arcana) check. The DC for this check equals 10 + the spell's level x 2 or 10 + the creature's Challenge Rating, whichever is higher. For spells originating from a magic item, use the spell's level; if the item's effect is not a spell with a discrete level, the DM determines an equivalent spell level for the DC calculation.

If successful, you learn the spell or innate magical ability. You can cast learned spells using your thaumaphage spellcasting feature by expending the appropriate amount of mana. Learned innate magical abilities can be replicated by expending mana equal to what a spell of a similar power level would cost, determined by the DM and typically based on an equivalent spell level.

You can know a number of learned spells and abilities equal to your Wisdom modifier + half your Thaumaphage level, rounded down, minimum 1. If you attempt to learn a new one while at your maximum, you must choose one to forget.
This ability can be used to learn spells from spellcasters or magic items, or innate magical abilities from creatures. It cannot be used to learn effects from other environmental magical sources.

subclassFeature.name: Mana Overflow
entries: Beginning at 10th level, when you gain mana from any source that would cause you to exceed your maximum mana capacity, instead of losing the excess, you can immediately cast a spell you know without expending mana.
The spell must have a mana cost equal to or less than the excess mana you would have gained. You can use this feature once per long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Conversion
entries: At 14th level, you achieve a new height of control over magical energies, allowing you to convert your magical knowledge or existing spell energy into raw mana.

As a bonus action, you can choose one spell you know through your Mana Shaping feature and temporarily unlearn it until you finish a long rest. When you do so, you regain mana equal to half the spell's base mana cost, rounded down. You can use this ability a number of times equal to your Wisdom modifier, minimum once, per long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Warrior's Path
entries: Starting when you choose this art at 2nd level, your training focuses on physical combat enhanced by magical energy.
You gain proficiency in Athletics if you don't already have it. Additionally, you gain proficiency with either one martial weapon of your choice or shields.

subclassFeature.name: Empowered Form
entries: Starting at 2nd level, you can consume mana to convert your stored magical energy into temporary physical enhancements. These enhancements last for 1 hour and don't stack with magic items or spells that provide similar benefits.

You can only have one Mana Enhancement active at any given time. If you activate a new Mana Enhancement while another is active, the previous one ends. Enhanced ability score modifiers affect your statistics normally while active, but your enhanced ability scores cannot exceed 20 + your Constitution modifier, regardless of other bonuses.

items: {@b Strength Enhancement:} You can spend mana to enhance your Strength modifier. For every 2 mana you spend, you gain a +1 bonus to your Strength modifier. The maximum bonus you can gain from this enhancement is equal to half your proficiency bonus, rounded up.

{@b Dexterity Enhancement:} You can spend mana to enhance your Dexterity modifier. For every 2 mana you spend, you gain a +1 bonus to your Dexterity modifier. The maximum bonus you can gain from this enhancement is equal to half your proficiency bonus, rounded up.

{@b Constitution Enhancement:} You can spend mana to enhance your Constitution modifier. For every 2 mana you spend, you gain a +1 bonus to your Constitution modifier. The maximum bonus you can gain from this enhancement is equal to half your proficiency bonus, rounded up.

subclassFeature.name: Adaptive Surge
entries: Starting at 6th level, you unlock new ways to channel your mana for potent, immediate effects. You can use the following abilities by expending mana as specified:

items: {@b Kinetic Weave:} You can spend 1 mana as a bonus action to take the Dash or Disengage action.

{@b Resilient Hide:} You can use your bonus action and spend 2 mana to gain temporary hit points equal to your Thaumaphage level. These temporary hit points last for 1 minute or until they are replaced by new temporary hit points.

{@b Surging Might:} When you hit a creature with a melee weapon attack, you can choose to spend 2 mana to double the number of weapon damage dice rolled for that attack. This effect applies only to the weapon's base damage dice. You can use this option once per attack.

subclassFeature.name: Shared Vigor
entries: Starting at 10th level, you can channel your vitalizing mana to empower an ally. As an action, you can touch one willing creature and expend mana to grant them one of the benefits of your Empowered Form feature: Strength Enhancement, Dexterity Enhancement, or Constitution Enhancement.
The mana cost, duration of 1 hour, and maximum bonus of half your proficiency bonus are identical to when you use Empowered Form on yourself.

While an enhancement you granted is active on another creature via this feature, you cannot benefit from your own Empowered Form feature. Activating Empowered Form on yourself causes any enhancement you previously granted to another creature via this feature to end immediately. Similarly, granting an enhancement to a creature via this feature ends any Empowered Form currently active on yourself.

subclassFeature.name: Mind Over Flesh
entries: Beginning at 14th level, your mastery over mana allows you to sustain multiple augmentations with greater ease.

The restriction from Empowered Form that you can only have one enhancement active at a time is modified: you can now have multiple different Mana Enhancements, Strength, Dexterity, and/or Constitution, active simultaneously on yourself. You must pay the mana cost for each enhancement you activate and maintain on yourself.

Additionally, while you have one or more enhancements active on yourself, you can also maintain one enhancement on a single willing creature using your Shared Vigor feature. If you grant an enhancement to a different ally using Shared Vigor, the enhancement on the previous ally ends, but your personal enhancements remain unaffected.
The abilities granted by your Adaptive Surge feature can be used as described, by expending mana for their effects, regardless of which Mana Enhancements are active.

spell.name: Convert Essence
entries: You touch a non-magical object and transmute your own vitality into raw magical potential, storing it within the item. You expend one of your Hit Dice, rolling it without regaining any hit points. The object gains a number of temporary magical charges equal to the number you rolled.

For the duration, any creature holding the object can use an action to draw on its power, expending any number of charges to gain temporary hit points. The number of temporary hit points gained is equal to the number of charges expended.
The charges fade after 1 minute or when all have been used. An object can only hold charges from one casting of this spell at a time.

entriesHigherLevel.name: At Higher Levels
entries: The number of temporary hit points gained per charge increases as you gain levels. At 5th level, you gain 2 temporary hit points for each charge expended. This increases to 3 temporary hit points at 11th level, and 4 temporary hit points at 17th level.

spell.name: Ether Burn
entries: You unleash a blast of raw magic. Choose one of the following areas of effect originating from you: a 15-foot cone, a 10-foot radius sphere, or a 30-foot line that is 5 feet wide.

Roll 4d6. The result is the spell's total force damage. This damage is divided evenly among all creatures within the chosen area, rounded down. Each affected creature must make a Dexterity saving throw. On a successful save, a creature takes half of its allocated share of the damage.

entriesHigherLevel.name: At Higher Levels
entries: When you cast this spell using additional mana, 2nd level or higher, the spell's total damage increases by 1d6 for each level above 1st.

item.name: Mana Crystal
entries: This crystal, often intricately carved or naturally formed with unique patterns, serves as a Thaumaphage's primary tool for storing and channeling magical energy. While attuned to this crystal, you can use it as a spellcasting focus for your Thaumaphage spells.

The crystal has charges. The maximum number of charges the crystal can hold is equal to your current maximum mana capacity, as determined by your Thaumaphage class features. The crystal regains all its expended charges when you finish a long rest.
As an action, you can expend any number of charges from your Mana Crystal to gain an equal amount of mana, 1 charge = 1 mana, up to your maximum mana capacity.
If your Mana Crystal is lost or destroyed, you can craft a replacement during a long rest by expending 50 gp worth of materials and imbuing a suitable crystal with your own magical essence.
